mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and lower recession risk, increasing expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% from at or below 2.0%, tighter short-rate conditions raise equity discount rates and reduce risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0% after being at or below 2.0% in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the triggered downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS combined with contained CPIAUCSL indicates real consumption growth rather than nominal price pass-through, supporting earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive while CPIAUCSL 3-month growth is below its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction has a nonpositive coefficient for 60-day returns or if triggered cases fail to beat unconditional returns by at least 25 bps on average.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in INDPRO signals weakening production and margin pressure, reducing near-term earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO decline triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below 1100 thousand units, residential investment and credit creation are contracting, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1100 thousand units after being at or above 1100 in the prior three months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1100 triggers generate positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the downside hit rate is below 55% after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate together with a rising UNRATE indicates restrictive policy colliding with labor-market deterioration, increasing recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is greater than 0 and UNRATE 3-month change is greater than 0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE trigger does not produce lower 20-day returns than either single-series trigger alone or if its average forward return is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive four-quarter trend in GDPC1 signals broad real demand growth and supports aggregate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive four-quarter GDPC1 growth is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative or flat GDPC1 growth in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration exceeds its trailing trend, investors price higher inflation risk and tighter policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its 12-month growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration triggers have positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the triggered negative-return frequency is not above 55%.
